title: Cheilanthes belangeri
taxonomy: Cheilanthes belangeri is a species of the genus Cheilanthes, which belongs to the family Pteridaceae. The family Pteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the shield ferns or maidenhair ferns. Cheilanthes belangeri is further classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a group of vascular plants that are characterized by the presence of true leaves and a vascular system. This classification places Cheilanthes belangeri among the ferns that reproduce via spores.
uses: Cheilanthes belangeri is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia. The leaves of Cheilanthes belangeri have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties. In some traditional medicine practices, the leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties are also believed to contribute to its medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cheilanthes belangeri for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cheilanthes belangeri, also known as the Belanger's lip fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues.
